description: Building shell commands from untrusted strings risks injection.
body: Use argument arrays or avoid shell. Use when a proposed approach skips trusted boundaries, dangerous sinks, authorization checks, secret flows, and negative-path tests or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in server-side deny tests, sink-specific payload tests, secret redaction checks, dependency evidence, and audit notes. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause client-only enforcement, secret disclosure, injection, permissive fallback, and unsafe shortcuts.
